title: Argo OHC Sampling Bias — North Atlantic Current
text:
Reanalysis-based simulation study

Use the reanalysis GLORYS12 for synthetic velocity and temperature data
Advect virtual Argo floats with the GLORYS velocity field using the PARCELS virtualfleet package
“Observe” the temperature profile at each float’s surfacing position
Estimate OHC from the virtual floats within grid cells
Compare to the true OHC integrated from the full GLORYS field
Quantify the bias directly

This report. Deploy a grid of virtual floats in the North Atlantic Current where there is a strong current and temperature gradient and compute bias at different grid sizes


Setup — imports and experiment configuration
import sys, os
sys.path.insert(0, os.path.abspath("../code"))

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import xarray as xr
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import ohc
import ohc_bias
from trajplots import (open_trajectories, map_trajectories_minimal,
                       map_positions_by_month, map_point_trajectories,
                       plot_depth_profiles, map_field, map_fields_row)

# --- experiment configuration (mirrors code/configs/nac_gs_1yr_wide.json) ---
TRAJ            = "../data/virtualfleet/nac_gs_1yr_wide_task*.zarr"  # batch trajectory zarrs
VELOCITY        = "../data/velocity_gs_wide/velocity_*.nc"          # GLORYS uo/vo/thetao (extended)
TRUTH_BOUNDS    = (30, 48, -74, -49)    # advection domain (lat0, lat1, lon0, lon1)
DEPLOY_BOUNDS   = (36, 40, -68, -62)    # float deployment box (4° lat × 6° lon) = analysis region
ANALYSIS_BOUNDS = DEPLOY_BOUNDS
DEGS            = [1.0, 0.5, 1/12]      # estimator cell sizes to compare
MAP_DEG         = 0.5                   # cell size for the monthly maps
DEPTHS          = ["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"]   # both depth layers, shown throughout

title: Argo OHC Sampling Bias — North Atlantic Current
text:
6.1 Background
Now we interpolate the sparse virtual-float OHC anomalies onto the full analysis-domain grid at the native 1/12° resolution, using a Gaussian process model fit with GpGp. The predicted anomaly field is added back to the climatological mean to give an absolute-OHC field directly comparable to the GLORYS truth on the same native grid.
code/ohc_gp_interp.R fits one GP per depth (0-700m, 0-2000m) with the stationary spatiotemporal covariance function matern_spheretime, pooling all ~150 profiles across the year, with covariance parameters \(\theta\) = (\(\sigma^2\), \(\rho_s\), \(\rho_t\), \(\nu\), \(\tau^2\)) = (variance, spatial range, temporal range, smoothness, nugget).
The matern_spheretime function embeds the (lat, lon) into 3D coordinates, and then inputs the resulting locations into the matern_spacetime covariance function wtih parameterization
\[
k_{\theta}(x, y) = \sigma^2\,\frac{2^{1-\nu}}{\Gamma(\nu)}
  (\|D^{-1}(x-y)\|)^\nu\,K_\nu\!\left(\|D^{-1}(x-y)\|\right)
  + \sigma^2\tau^2\,\mathbf{1}_{x = y},
\]
where \(D = \operatorname{diag}(\rho_s,\rho_s,\rho_s,\rho_t)\) and \(K_\nu\) is the modified Bessel function of the second kind.
Smoothness is fixed at \(\nu = \tfrac{3}{2}\), such that the function is once differentiable. Note, If the smoothness parameter is free the Fisher scoring algorithm does not converge.
When \(\nu = \tfrac{3}{2}\), the covariance function has the closed form \((1+r)\,e^{-r}\) where \(r = \|D^{-1}(s-s')\|\).






NoteDerivation: Matérn \(\nu=\tfrac{3}{2}\) closed form





The general Matérn covariance (dropping \(\sigma^2\) and the nugget) is
\[
k(r) = \frac{2^{1-\nu}}{\Gamma(\nu)}\,r^{\nu}\,K_{\nu}(r), \qquad r \geq 0.
\]
For \(\nu = p + \tfrac{1}{2}\) with \(p\) a non-negative integer, \(K_\nu\) reduces to an elementary function via the identity
\[
K_{p+1/2}(r) = \sqrt{\frac{\pi}{2r}}\,e^{-r}\sum_{k=0}^{p}\frac{(p+k)!}{k!\,(p-k)!}\,(2r)^{-k}.
\]
Setting \(p = 1\) (so \(\nu = \tfrac{3}{2}\)) gives \(K_{3/2}(r) = \sqrt{\tfrac{\pi}{2r}}\,e^{-r}(1 + r^{-1})\), i.e. \(K_{3/2}(r) = \sqrt{\tfrac{\pi}{2}}\,r^{-3/2}\,e^{-r}(r+1)\).
Substituting into the general form:
\[
k(r) = \frac{2^{1-3/2}}{\Gamma(3/2)}\,r^{3/2}\cdot\sqrt{\tfrac{\pi}{2}}\,r^{-3/2}\,e^{-r}(1+r)
      = \frac{2^{-1/2}}{\tfrac{\sqrt{\pi}}{2}}\cdot\frac{\sqrt{\pi}}{\sqrt{2}}\,e^{-r}(1+r).
\]
Therefore
\[
\boxed{k(r) = (1+r)\,e^{-r}},
\]
where \(r = \|D^{-1}(s-s')\|\).

title: Argo OHC Sampling Bias — North Atlantic Current
text:
6.2 Model Fit
GpGp::fit_model fits the mean parameter \(\hat\beta\) (fit$betahat) and the matern_spheretime covariance parameters.
Asymptotic standard errors from fit$info (log scale) are transformed back to natural scale with the delta method: \({\mathrm{se}}(\theta) \approx \theta\, {\mathrm{se}}(\log\theta)\).


Per-depth fit diagnostics: log-likelihood, convergence, profile count
sim_anom = ohc_clim.float_ohc_anomalies(sim, clim_field)

_GP_DIR = "../data/gp_interp"
os.makedirs(_GP_DIR, exist_ok=True)
_profiles_csv    = f"{_GP_DIR}/profiles_anom.csv"
_fit_summary_csv = f"{_GP_DIR}/gp_fit_summary.csv"
_model_cache     = f"{_GP_DIR}/gp_model_cache.rds"  # fitted model shared across grids

_keys = ["month", "cell_lat", "cell_lon"]
_truth_monthly = truth_field.resample(time="1MS").mean().rename({"time": "month"})
truth_native   = truth_by_deg[1 / 12]
# Climatological mean field on the native grid -- reused by Levitus tabsets below.
mean_field_cells = ohc_clim.climatology_cells_table(
    clim_field, truth_field["latitude"].values, truth_field["longitude"].values,
    months=_truth_monthly["month"].values,
)

# Temporal prediction point within each month: "first" (1st), "middle" (15th), "last" (last day).
_GP_MONTH_DAY = "middle"

# Prediction resolutions -- add entries here to predict on additional grids.
_GP_RES_LABELS  = {1/12: "1/12° (native)", 0.5: "1/2°", 1.0: "1°"}
_pred_grid_csvs = {deg: f"{_GP_DIR}/pred_grid_{deg:.4g}deg.csv"    for deg in [1/12, 1.0]}
_virt_out_csvs  = {deg: f"{_GP_DIR}/gp_predictions_{deg:.4g}deg.csv" for deg in [1/12, 1.0]}

def _grid_lats_lons(deg):
    if deg < 0.1:
        return truth_field["latitude"].values, truth_field["longitude"].values
    return (np.sort(truth_by_deg[deg]["cell_lat"].unique()),
            np.sort(truth_by_deg[deg]["cell_lon"].unique()))

for deg, grid_csv in _pred_grid_csvs.items():
    lats, lons = _grid_lats_lons(deg)
    ohc_clim.build_pred_grid(lats, lons).to_csv(grid_csv, index=False)

ohc_clim.write_profile_csv(sim_anom, _profiles_csv)
# First iteration fits the model and saves it to cache; subsequent iterations load it.
for deg, out_csv in _virt_out_csvs.items():
    ohc_clim.run_gp_interp(
        _profiles_csv, _pred_grid_csvs[deg], out_csv, _fit_summary_csv,
        model_cache=_model_cache, month_day=_GP_MONTH_DAY,
    )

gp_fit_summary = ohc_clim.load_gp_fit_summary(_fit_summary_csv)
gp_fit_summary.groupby("depth")[["loglik", "converged", "n_obs"]].first()

Build float OHC anomalies and run the GpGp interpolation
def build_gp_res_ctx(out_csv, deg, profiles_df=None):
    """Load GP predictions from out_csv and assemble the display context for resolution deg."""
    lats, lons = _grid_lats_lons(deg)
    anom_field = ohc_clim.load_gp_anomaly_field(out_csv, lats, lons)
    gp_field   = ohc_clim.assemble_gp_field(anom_field, clim_field)
    cells      = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(gp_field)
    se_cells   = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(gp_field,   value_cols=("ohc_700_se",   "ohc_2000_se"))
    anom_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(anom_field, value_cols=("ohc_700_anom", "ohc_2000_anom"))
    mean_cells = ohc_clim.climatology_cells_table(clim_field, lats, lons, months=gp_field["month"].values)
    obs_anom_cells = (
        ohc.grid_cells(profiles_df, ["ohc_700_anom", "ohc_2000_anom"], deg=1/12)
        if profiles_df is not None else None
    )
    if deg < 0.1:
        _bf = (gp_field[list(DEPTHS)] - _truth_monthly[list(DEPTHS)]).rename(
            {d: f"{d}_bias" for d in DEPTHS}
        )
        bias_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(_bf, value_cols=[f"{d}_bias" for d in DEPTHS])
    else:
        _merged = cells.merge(
            truth_by_deg[deg][_keys + list(DEPTHS)].rename(columns={d: f"{d}_truth" for d in DEPTHS}),
            on=_keys, how="left",
        )
        bias_cells = _merged.copy()
        for _d in DEPTHS:
            bias_cells[f"{_d}_bias"] = bias_cells[_d] - bias_cells[f"{_d}_truth"]
    return dict(
        cells=cells, se_cells=se_cells, anom_cells=anom_cells,
        obs_anom_cells=obs_anom_cells, bias_cells=bias_cells,
        mean_cells=mean_cells, truth_cells=truth_by_deg[deg],
        obs_profiles=profiles_df,
    )

def build_gp_ctx(out_csvs, profiles_df=None):
    """Build the {res_label: context} dict used by emit_gp_map_tabset / emit_gp_violin_tabset."""
    return {_GP_RES_LABELS[deg]: build_gp_res_ctx(out_csv, deg, profiles_df) for deg, out_csv in out_csvs.items()}

_virtual_ctx = build_gp_ctx(_virt_out_csvs, profiles_df=sim_anom)




Format the pooled GP fit parameter table for display
def _fmt_fit_summary(df):
    """One row per parameter, columns 'estimate ± SE' per depth, in display units."""
    d = df.copy()
    d["display"] = d.apply(
        lambda r: f"{r['estimate']:.4g}" if pd.isna(r["std_error"])
        else f"{r['estimate']:.4g} ± {r['std_error']:.3g}",
        axis=1,
    )
    wide = d.pivot(index="parameter", columns="depth", values="display")
    order = ["mean (intercept)", "variance", "spatial_range", "temporal_range", "smoothness", "nugget"]
    return wide.reindex([p for p in order if p in wide.index])

# Spatial range: radians (sphere of radius 1) -> km. Temporal range: already in
# days (day-of-year coordinate). Mean/variance/nugget: J/m2 (or squared) -> GJ/m2
# (or squared). nugget in GpGp's matern_spheretime is a *relative* fraction of
# variance -- K(0) = variance * (1 + nugget) (verified directly against
# matern_spheretime's output) -- so it's dimensionless, unlike variance itself;
# no GJ rescaling.
EARTH_RADIUS_KM = 6371.0
_scale = {
    "mean (intercept)": ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, "variance": ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ ** 2,
    "spatial_range": EARTH_RADIUS_KM, "temporal_range": 1.0,
    "smoothness": 1.0, "nugget": 1.0,
}
_disp = gp_fit_summary.copy()
_disp["estimate"] = _disp.apply(lambda r: r["estimate"] * _scale[r["parameter"]], axis=1)
_disp["std_error"] = _disp.apply(lambda r: r["std_error"] * _scale[r["parameter"]], axis=1)
fit_table = _fmt_fit_summary(_disp)
fit_table.index.name = "parameter"
fit_table

title: Argo OHC Sampling Bias — North Atlantic Current
text:
6.3 Predictions
Use GpGp’s machinery but perform simple kriging with an estimated mean by conditioning every prediction point on all observed profiles, rather than on a subset of neighbors.
Predictions are made at the center of grid cells on the 15th of each month (configurable via _GP_MONTH_DAY: "first", "middle", or "last").






Notekriging_predict logic





Let \(y \in \mathbb{R}^{n}\) be all of that depth’s profile OHC anomalies pooled across the year, at space-time locations \(s_1, \dots, s_n\) (fit$y, fit$locs), and let \(s_0\) be one prediction point.
kriging_predict sets the neighbour count to \(n\) (all pooled profiles).
Writing \(K \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\) for the observation covariance matrix \(K_{ij} = k_{\hat\theta}(s_i, s_j)\), \(k_0 \in \mathbb{R}^n\) for the observation-to-prediction cross-covariance \(k_{0,i} = k_{\hat\theta}(s_i, s_0)\), and \(k_{00} = k_{\hat\theta}(s_0, s_0)\) the prior variance at \(s_0\), the classical kriging predictor is
\[
\hat y(s_0) = \hat\beta + b^\top\,(y - \hat\beta \mathbf{1}_n),
\qquad b = K^{-1}k_0,
\qquad
\widehat{\mathrm{Var}}\big[y(s_0)\big] = k_{00} - k_0^\top K^{-1} k_0 .
\]
kriging_predict calls Linv_all <- vecchia_Linv(covparms, covfun_name, locs_all, NNarray, start_ind) factors for the Cholesky factorization \(K = LL^\top\). For the prediction row, directly returns both the kriging weight vector \(b\) and the conditional standard deviation \(\sigma_0 = \sqrt{k_{00} - k_0^\top b}\), packed into one sparse row
\[
\texttt{Linv\_all[i, ]} = \big[\,1/\sigma_0,\ -b_1/\sigma_0,\ \dots,\ -b_n/\sigma_0\,\big],
\]
such that
\[
b_k = -\frac{\texttt{Linv\_all[i,k+1]}}{\texttt{Linv\_all[i,1]}},
\qquad
\hat y(s_0) = \hat\beta + \sum_{k=1}^n b_k\,(y_k - \hat\beta),
\qquad
\widehat{\mathrm{Var}}\big[y(s_0)\big] = \frac{1}{(\texttt{Linv\_all[i,1]})^2}.
\]





GP-interpolated field vs native-grid truth, its standard error, and the GP - truth bias map
def make_gp_map(c, kind, d, res_label):
    """Render one map panel from a resolution context dict c."""
    if kind == "gp_mean":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["mean_cells"], d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"Climatological mean field, {d} by month ({res_label})")
    if kind == "obs_anom":
        _lats, _lons = _grid_lats_lons(1/12)
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["obs_anom_cells"], f"{d}_anom", vmin=-ANOM_VLIM[d], vmax=ANOM_VLIM[d], cmap="RdBu_r",
            cbar_label=f"{d} anomaly (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", scatter_dots=True, width=TABSET_FIGW,
            lats=_lats, lons=_lons,
            title=f"Observed Argo {d} anomaly by month ({res_label})")
    if kind == "gp":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["cells"], d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"GP-interpolated {d} by month ({res_label})")
    if kind == "gp_anom":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["anom_cells"], f"{d}_anom", vmin=-ANOM_VLIM[d], vmax=ANOM_VLIM[d], cmap="RdBu_r",
            cbar_label=f"{d} anomaly (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"GP-interpolated {d} anomaly by month ({res_label})")
    if kind == "gp_se":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["se_cells"], f"{d}_se", vmin=0, cmap="viridis", width=TABSET_FIGW,
            cbar_label=f"{d} GP standard error (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)",
            title=f"GP standard error, {d} ({res_label})")
    if kind == "truth_native":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["truth_cells"], d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"GLORYS truth {d} by month ({res_label})")
    if kind == "gp_bias":
        bcol = f"{d}_bias"
        bmax = float(c["bias_cells"][bcol].abs().max()) * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            c["bias_cells"], bcol, vmin=-bmax, vmax=bmax, cmap="RdBu_r",
            cbar_label=f"{d} bias (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"GP - truth bias, {d} ({res_label})")

GP_MAP_KINDS = [("gp_mean", "Mean field (climatology)"),
                ("obs_anom", "Observed Argo anomaly"),
                ("obs_anom_table", "Observed Argo anomaly table"),
                ("gp_anom", "GP-interpolated Argo anomaly"),
                ("gp", "GP-interpolated Argo ohc"),
                ("gp_se", "GP standard error"),
                ("truth_native", "GLORYS truth"),
                ("gp_bias", "GP - truth bias")]

def make_gp_table(c, d):
    profiles = c.get("obs_profiles")
    if profiles is None:
        return None
    col = f"{d}_anom"
    s = profiles[col]
    grp = profiles.groupby(pd.to_datetime(profiles["date"]).dt.to_period("M"))[col]
    raw = grp.agg(["count", "mean", "std", "min", "max"])
    tbl = raw.assign(
        mean=(raw["mean"] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).round(3),
        std=(raw["std"]   * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).round(3),
        min=(raw["min"]   * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).round(3),
        max=(raw["max"]   * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ).round(3),
    ).rename(columns={"count": "n", "std": "sd"})
    tbl.index = tbl.index.astype(str)
    tbl.index.name = "month"
    year_row = pd.DataFrame([{
        "n":    s.count(),
        "mean": round(s.mean() * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, 3),
        "sd":   round(s.std()  * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, 3),
        "min":  round(s.min()  * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, 3),
        "max":  round(s.max()  * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ, 3),
    }], index=pd.Index(["Year"], name="month"))
    return pd.concat([tbl, year_row])

def emit_gp_map_tabset(ctx):
    """Emit depth → resolution → map-kind tabset from a {res_label: c} context dict."""
    _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
    for d in DEPTHS:
        _emit(f"## {DEPTH_LABEL[d]}")
        _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
        for res_label, c in ctx.items():
            _emit(f"### {res_label}")
            _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
            for kind, label in GP_MAP_KINDS:
                _emit(f"#### {label}")
                if kind == "obs_anom_table":
                    tbl = make_gp_table(c, d)
                    if tbl is not None:
                        display(tbl)
                else:
                    _show_fig(make_gp_map(c, kind, d, res_label))
            _emit(":::")
        _emit(":::")
    _emit(":::")

def emit_gp_violin_tabset(ctx):
    """Emit depth → resolution violin tabset from a {res_label: c} context dict."""
    _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
    for d in DEPTHS:
        _emit(f"## {DEPTH_LABEL[d]}")
        _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
        for res_label, c in ctx.items():
            _emit(f"### {res_label}")
            _show_fig(ohc_bias.plot_bias_se_violin(
                c["bias_cells"], c["se_cells"], f"{d}_bias", f"{d}_se",
                title=f"{d} anomaly: GP bias vs SE ({res_label})",
            ))
        _emit(":::")
    _emit(":::")

emit_gp_map_tabset(_virtual_ctx)

title: Argo OHC Sampling Bias — North Atlantic Current
text:
9.1 Background
Now we perform anomaly interpolation with the fixed-radius Gaussian-weighted kernel smoother described in literature/levitus_2012_aux.tex (the Levitus objective-analysis scheme). The predicted anomaly is \(\hat A(s_0)\) at a point \(s_0\) with nearby profile anomalies \(Q_n\) at distance \(r_n\):
\[
w_n = \exp\!\left(-E\,\frac{r_n^2}{R^2}\right) \text{ for } r_n \le R,\ \text{else } 0,
\qquad E = 4,
\]
\[
\hat A(s_0) = \sum_n \lambda_n Q_n, \qquad \lambda_n = \frac{w_n}{\sum_m w_m}.
\]
Radius. Levitus sets \(R = 666\) km close to the size of this entire ~500 km x 650 km analysis domain
Interpolation standard errors The Levitus appendix derives a standard error for the analyzed value with a general formula for error propagation (GFEP), then simplifies using the Schwarz inequality bound on cross-covariances.
Their equations appear to imply that \(\sigma_A\), the standard error for an interpolated anomaly, is equal to \(\sigma_0\), the standard deviation of the weighted mean anomaly values within a given influence region.
I compute two standard errors, se_O, which assumes \(Corr(C_n, C_m) = 1\) for grid points \(n\) and \(m\) within the same influence region, and se_a, which alternatively assumes \(Cov(C_n, C_m) = 0\). \[
\texttt{se\_0} = \sigma_0,
\]
\[
\texttt{se\_a} = \sigma_0\sqrt{\textstyle\sum_n \lambda_n^2}
\qquad\text{(dropping the cross-terms)},
\]






NoteSource derivation: Levitus et al. 2012 appendix





Notation. At prediction gridpoint \((i,j)\), let \(N\) be the number of 1°-square mean anomalies \(Q_n\) within the influence radius, with weights \(w_n = \exp(-4\,r_n^2/R^2)\) and \(W = \sum_n w_n\). Define the correction contribution from the \(n\)th square, \[
C_n = \frac{w_n\,Q_n}{W},
\] so the analyzed anomaly is \(A = F + \sum_n C_n\) (first-guess \(F = 0\) for anomaly fields).
General formula for error propagation (GFEP) . Treating \(\partial A/\partial Q_n = w_n/W\): \[
\sigma_A = \sqrt{
  \sum_{n=1}^{N}\!\left(\frac{w_n}{W}\,\sigma_{C_n}\right)^{\!2}
  +\;
  \frac{2}{W^2}\sum_{n=1}^{N-1}\sum_{m=n+1}^{N} w_n w_m\,\sigma_{C_{n,m}}
},
\] where \(\sigma_{C_n}\) is the standard deviation of anomalies in the \(n\)th square and \(\sigma_{C_{n,m}}\) is the cross-covariance between squares \(n\) and \(m\).
The standard deviation (\(\sigma_0\)) of all observed ODSQ mean anomalies within the influence region surrounding an ODSQ at gridpoint \((i,j)\) is defined as:
\[
\sigma_0 = \sqrt{\frac{1}{N-1}\sum_{n=1}^{N}\!\left(C_n - \bar{C}\right)^2}.
\]
The cross covariance \(\sigma_{C_{n,m}}\) in can be replaced by the quantity \(\sigma_{C_n}\sigma_{C_m}\) because the Schwarz inequality guarantees that \(\left|\sigma_{C_{n,m}}\right| \le \sigma_{C_n}\sigma_{C_m}\).
Next we assume in equation that \(\sigma_{C_n} = \sigma_0\) and we have the standard error of the mean of the objectively analyzed value:
\[
\sigma_A = \frac{\sigma_0}{W}\sqrt{\sum_{n=1}^{N} w_n^2 + 2\!\sum_{n<m} w_n w_m}.
\]
The expression under the root is the binomial expansion of \(W^2 = \bigl(\sum_n w_n\bigr)^2\), so it reduces to \(\sigma_A = \sigma_0\).

title: Argo OHC Sampling Bias — North Atlantic Current
text:
9.2 Monthly predictions


Run the Levitus-style kernel-smoother interpolation (code/ohc_levitus_interp.R)
#LEVITUS_R_KM = ohc_clim.LEVITUS_R_DEFAULT_KM
LEVITUS_R_KM = 666
_levitus_out_csv = f"{_GP_DIR}/levitus_predictions.csv"

# Same profiles/grid CSVs already written for the GP path -- both estimators
# read the identical input format.
ohc_clim.run_levitus_interp(_profiles_csv, _pred_grid_csvs[1/12], _levitus_out_csv, R=LEVITUS_R_KM)

levitus_anom_field = ohc_clim.load_levitus_anomaly_field(
    _levitus_out_csv, truth_field["latitude"].values, truth_field["longitude"].values
)
ohc_levitus_field = ohc_clim.assemble_levitus_field(levitus_anom_field, clim_field)

levitus_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(ohc_levitus_field)
# The anomaly is what the kernel smoother actually outputs; ohc_levitus_field/
# levitus_cells above is that anomaly plus the climatology added back.
levitus_anom_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(
    levitus_anom_field, value_cols=("ohc_700_anom", "ohc_2000_anom")
)
levitus_se_a_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(
    ohc_levitus_field, value_cols=("ohc_700_se_a", "ohc_2000_se_a")
)
levitus_se_0_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(
    ohc_levitus_field, value_cols=("ohc_700_se_0", "ohc_2000_se_0")
)

_levitus_bias_field = (ohc_levitus_field[["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"]] - _truth_monthly[["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"]]).rename(
    {"ohc_700": "ohc_700_bias", "ohc_2000": "ohc_2000_bias"}
)
levitus_bias_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(_levitus_bias_field, value_cols=("ohc_700_bias", "ohc_2000_bias"))


wrote 0 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/levitus_predictions.csv 




Levitus-interpolated field vs native-grid truth, both standard errors, and the bias map
def show_levitus_map(kind, d):
    if kind == "levitus_mean":
        # Same climatology, same prediction grid/months as the GP tabset above.
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            mean_field_cells, d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"Climatological mean field, {d} by month")
    if kind == "obs_anom":
        _obs_cells = _virtual_ctx["1/12° (native)"]["obs_anom_cells"]
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            _obs_cells, f"{d}_anom", vmin=-ANOM_VLIM[d], vmax=ANOM_VLIM[d], cmap="RdBu_r",
            cbar_label=f"{d} anomaly (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", scatter_dots=True, width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"Observed Argo {d} anomaly by month")
    if kind == "levitus":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            levitus_cells, d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"Levitus-interpolated Argo {d} by month (R={LEVITUS_R_KM:.0f} km)")
    if kind == "levitus_anom":
        acol = f"{d}_anom"
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            levitus_anom_cells, acol, vmin=-ANOM_VLIM[d], vmax=ANOM_VLIM[d], cmap="RdBu_r",
            cbar_label=f"{d} anomaly (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"Levitus-interpolated Argo {d} anomaly by month (R={LEVITUS_R_KM:.0f} km)")
    if kind == "levitus_se_a":
        scol = f"{d}_se_a"
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            levitus_se_a_cells, scol, vmin=0, cmap="viridis", width=TABSET_FIGW,
            cbar_label=f"{d} SE_a (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)",
            title=f"Levitus se_a, {d} (R={LEVITUS_R_KM:.0f} km)")
    if kind == "levitus_se_0":
        scol = f"{d}_se_0"
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            levitus_se_0_cells, scol, vmin=0, cmap="viridis", width=TABSET_FIGW,
            cbar_label=f"{d} SE_0 (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)",
            title=f"Levitus se_0, {d} (R={LEVITUS_R_KM:.0f} km)")
    if kind == "truth_native":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            truth_native, d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"GLORYS truth {d} by month (native 1/12°)")
    if kind == "levitus_bias":
        bcol = f"{d}_bias"
        bmax = float(levitus_bias_cells[bcol].abs().quantile(0.95)) * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            levitus_bias_cells, bcol, vmin=-bmax, vmax=bmax, cmap="RdBu_r",
            cbar_label=f"{d} bias (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"Levitus - truth bias, {d} (R={LEVITUS_R_KM:.0f} km)")

_emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
for d in DEPTHS:
    _emit(f"## {DEPTH_LABEL[d]}")
    _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
    for kind, label in [("levitus_mean", "Mean field (climatology)"),
                         ("obs_anom", "Observed Argo anomaly"),
                         ("levitus_anom", "Levitus-interpolated Argo anomaly"),
                         ("levitus", "Levitus-interpolated Argo ohc"),
                         ("levitus_se_a", "se_a"),
                         ("levitus_se_0", "se_0"),
                         ("truth_native", "GLORYS truth (native)"),
                         ("levitus_bias", "Levitus - truth bias")]:
        _emit(f"### {label}")
        _show_fig(show_levitus_map(kind, d))
    _emit(":::")
_emit(":::")

    _show_fig(ohc_bias.plot_bias_se_violin(
        levitus_bias_cells,
        [levitus_se_a_cells, levitus_se_0_cells],
        f"{d}_bias",
        [f"{d}_se_a", f"{d}_se_0"],
        quantiles=[0.05,0.25,0.75,.95],

title: Argo OHC Sampling Bias — North Atlantic Current
text:
9.3 Pooled predictions
Pool all 2020 anomalies to interpolate anomaly field for the year and then add to monthly mean field to compute per-month bias


Run the pooled (whole-year) Levitus-style kernel-smoother interpolation
_levitus_pooled_csv = f"{_GP_DIR}/levitus_pooled_predictions.csv"
ohc_clim.run_levitus_interp(_profiles_csv, _pred_grid_csvs[1/12], _levitus_pooled_csv, R=LEVITUS_R_KM, pooled=True)

levitus_pooled_anom_field = ohc_clim.load_levitus_pooled_anomaly_field(
    _levitus_pooled_csv, truth_field["latitude"].values, truth_field["longitude"].values
)
ohc_levitus_pooled_field = ohc_clim.assemble_levitus_pooled_field(
    levitus_pooled_anom_field, clim_field, months=_truth_monthly["month"].values
)

levitus_pooled_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(ohc_levitus_pooled_field)
_levitus_pooled_bias_field = (
    ohc_levitus_pooled_field[["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"]] - _truth_monthly[["ohc_700", "ohc_2000"]]
).rename({"ohc_700": "ohc_700_bias", "ohc_2000": "ohc_2000_bias"})
levitus_pooled_bias_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(
    _levitus_pooled_bias_field, value_cols=("ohc_700_bias", "ohc_2000_bias")
)
levitus_pooled_se_a_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(
    ohc_levitus_pooled_field, value_cols=("ohc_700_se_a", "ohc_2000_se_a")
)
levitus_pooled_se_0_cells = ohc_clim.to_cells_table(
    ohc_levitus_pooled_field, value_cols=("ohc_700_se_0", "ohc_2000_se_0")
)


pooled: predicted 2 depth(s) from n=150 profiles (whole year)
wrote 3577 rows -> ../data/gp_interp/levitus_pooled_predictions.csv 




Pooled-Levitus field vs native-grid truth, both standard errors, and the bias map
_POOLED_MAP_KW = dict(extent=[-76, -47, 28, 50],
                      boxes=[(*TRUTH_BOUNDS, "advection domain", '0.5', "-"),
                             (*ANALYSIS_BOUNDS, "analysis domain")])

def show_levitus_pooled_map(kind, d):
    if kind == "levitus_pooled_mean":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            mean_field_cells, d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"Climatological mean field, {d} by month")
    if kind == "obs_anom":
        _obs_cells = _virtual_ctx["1/12° (native)"]["obs_anom_cells"]
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            _obs_cells, f"{d}_anom", vmin=-ANOM_VLIM[d], vmax=ANOM_VLIM[d], cmap="RdBu_r",
            cbar_label=f"{d} anomaly (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", scatter_dots=True, width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"Observed Argo {d} anomaly by month")
    if kind == "levitus_pooled_anom":
        # Static: one map for the whole year, not a monthly facet.
        acol = f"{d}_anom"
        fig, _ = map_fields_row(
            [levitus_pooled_anom_field[acol] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ], titles=[d],
            cmaps=["RdBu_r"], cbar_labels=[f"{d} anomaly (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)"],
            vmin=-ANOM_VLIM[d], vmax=ANOM_VLIM[d], show=False,
            suptitle=f"Pooled Levitus-interpolated Argo {d} anomaly, whole year (R={LEVITUS_R_KM:.0f} km)",
            **_POOLED_MAP_KW,
        )
        return fig
    if kind == "levitus_pooled":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            levitus_pooled_cells, d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"Pooled Levitus-interpolated Argo {d} by month (R={LEVITUS_R_KM:.0f} km)")
    if kind in ("levitus_pooled_se_a", "levitus_pooled_se_0"):
        suf = "_se_a" if kind == "levitus_pooled_se_a" else "_se_0"
        scol = f"{d}_anom{suf}"
        label = "SE_a" if suf == "_se_a" else "SE_0"
        fig, _ = map_fields_row(
            [levitus_pooled_anom_field[scol] * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ], titles=[d],
            cmaps=["viridis"], cbar_labels=[f"{d} {label} (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)"],
            vmin=0, show=False,
            suptitle=f"Pooled Levitus {label}, {d}, whole year (R={LEVITUS_R_KM:.0f} km)",
            **_POOLED_MAP_KW,
        )
        return fig
    if kind == "truth_native":
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            truth_native, d, vmin=VLIM[d][0], vmax=VLIM[d][1], width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"GLORYS truth {d} by month (native 1/12°)")
    if kind == "levitus_pooled_bias":
        bcol = f"{d}_bias"
        bmax = float(levitus_pooled_bias_cells[bcol].abs().quantile(0.95)) * ohc_bias.J_TO_GJ
        return ohc_bias.plot_monthly_cell_maps(
            levitus_pooled_bias_cells, bcol, vmin=-bmax, vmax=bmax, cmap="RdBu_r",
            cbar_label=f"{d} bias (GJ m$^{{-2}}$)", width=TABSET_FIGW,
            title=f"Pooled Levitus - truth bias, {d} (R={LEVITUS_R_KM:.0f} km)")

_emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
for d in DEPTHS:
    _emit(f"## {DEPTH_LABEL[d]}")
    _emit("::: {.panel-tabset}")
    for kind, label in [("levitus_pooled_mean", "Mean field (climatology)"),
                         ("obs_anom", "Observed Argo anomaly"),
                         ("levitus_pooled_anom", "Pooled Argo anomaly (whole year)"),
                         ("levitus_pooled", "Pooled Levitus-interpolated Argo ohc"),
                         ("levitus_pooled_se_a", "SE_a"),
                         ("levitus_pooled_se_0", "SE_0"),
                         ("truth_native", "GLORYS truth (native)"),
                         ("levitus_pooled_bias", "Pooled Levitus - truth bias")]:
        _emit(f"### {label}")
        _show_fig(show_levitus_pooled_map(kind, d))
    _emit(":::")
_emit(":::")

title: Argo OHC Sampling Bias
text:
This site collects analysis reports for a study of ocean heat content (OHC) sampling bias in Argo float observations, with a focus on the North Atlantic Current system.
The sampling bias arises because Argo floats are not uniformly distributed in space or time. We use GLORYS12 reanalysis fields and PARCELS Lagrangian particle simulations to characterize and quantify the bias.

title: Argo OHC Sampling Bias
text:
This site collects analysis reports for a study of ocean heat content (OHC) sampling bias in Argo float observations, with a focus on the North Atlantic Current system.
The sampling bias arises because Argo floats are not uniformly distributed in space or time. We use GLORYS12 reanalysis fields and PARCELS Lagrangian particle simulations to characterize and quantify the bias.

title: Argo OHC Sampling Bias

Data

Argo floats — gridded profiles from Argo GDAC
GLORYS12 — 1/12° global ocean reanalysis (Copernicus Marine)
PARCELS — Lagrangian ocean analysis framework
